content.problemStatement:
Arnold is planning to follow a diet suggested by his Nutritionist. The Nutritionist prescribed him the total protein, carbohydrates and fats he should take daily. Arnold searches on an online food store and makes a list of protein, carbohydrates and fats contained in a single unit of various food items.

His target is to have the maximum protein, carbohydrates and fats in his diet without exceeding the prescribed limit. He also wants to have as many diverse food items as much as possible. That is, he does not want to have many units of one food item and 000 of others. Multiple combinations of 'units of food items' are possible to achieve the target. Mathematically speaking, diversity is more if the difference between the number of units of food item chosen the most and the number of units of another food item chosen the least, is as small as possible.
To solve this problem, he uses maximum possible number of units of all the items so that total amount of protein, carbohydrates and fats is not more than prescribed limit. For example - if total nutrition required is 100P,130C100P, 130C100P,130C and 130F130F130F (where PPP is Protein, CCC is Carbohydrates and FFF is Fats) and 222 different food items, viz. Item AAA and Item BBB, have following amount of nutrition:

Item A−10P, 20C, 30FA - 10P,~20C,~30FA−10P, 20C, 30F

Item B−20P, 30C, 20FB - 20P,~30C,~20FB−20P, 30C, 20F

then, he can have (maximum possible) 222 units of all the items as having 333 units will exceed the prescribed amount of Carbohydrates and fats.

Next, he chooses food items to fulfill the remaining nutrients. He chooses one more units of maximum number of food items. He continues this process till he cannot add a unit of any food item to his diet without exceeding the prescribed limit. In this example, he can choose one more unit of item BBB or one more unit of item AAA. In case he has two sets of food items then the priority is given to fulfill the requirements of Protein, Carbohydrates, and Fats in that order. So he chooses item BBB.

You will be provided the maximum nutrients required and the nutrients available in various food items. You need to find the amount of nutrients for which there is a shortfall as compared to the prescription, after making his selection using the process described above. In the example he still needs 20P,0C,10F20P, 0C, 10F20P,0C,10F to achieve his target.


Input Format

First line contains the maximum limit of nutrients in the following format.
xP yC zFxP~yC~zFxP yC zF, where x,yx, yx,y and zzz are integers.
Second line contains nutrient composition of different food items separated by pipe (∣)(|)(∣).


Output Format
Print the shortfall for each nutrient type, fulfilled separated by space.

E.g. If the output is 10P,20C,30F,10P, 20C, 30F,10P,20C,30F, then print ‘‘10 20 30"``10~20~30"‘‘10 20 30" (without quotes)
constraints: Maximum amount of Nutrients is less than 1500 i.e. x+y+z≤1500x +y + z \leq 1500x+y+z≤1500
Amount of P,C,FP, C, FP,C,F in two food items will not be same
P,C,FP, C, FP,C,F values in input can be in any order
Output should be in order - P,C,FP, C, FP,C,F.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Having 222 units of item AAA and 333 units of item BBB provides -
2∗[10P 20C 30F]+3∗[20P 30C 20F]=100P, 130C, 120F.2 * [10P~20C~30F] + 3 * [20P~30C~20F] = 100P,~130C,~120F.2∗[10P 20C 30F]+3∗[20P 30C 20F]=100P, 130C, 120F.
This is the best combination that can reduce the shortfall [20P, 0C, 10F][20P,~0C,~10F][20P, 0C, 10F] without exceeding his prescription. In contrast, if 333 units of AAA and 222 units of BBB are chosen then 
3∗[10P 20C 30F]+2∗[20P 30C 20F]=70P, 120C, 130F3 * [10P~20C~30F] + 2 * [20P~30C~20F] = 70P,~120C,~130F3∗[10P 20C 30F]+2∗[20P 30C 20F]=70P, 120C, 130F 
produces a shortfall of [30P,10C,0F][30P, 10C, 0F][30P,10C,0F]. However, since protein shortfall in this case is more than the previous combination, this is not the right combination to follow.

Explanation:
Having 999 units of item AAA, 999 units of item BBB and 888 units of Item CCC provides - 
9∗[4P 9C 2F]+9∗[4P 3C 2F]+8∗[7P 1C 3F]=108P, 116C, 60F9 * [4P~9C~2F] + 9 * [4P~3C~2F] + 8 * [7P~1C~3F] = 108P,~116C,~60F9∗[4P 9C 2F]+9∗[4P 3C 2F]+8∗[7P 1C 3F]=108P, 116C, 60F.
This is the best combination that can reduce the shortfall [2P, 4C, 50F][2P,~4C,~50F][2P, 4C, 50F] without exceeding his prescription.
